name: Art's Spicy Turkey Chili
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the ground turkey and drain it, placing it in a large cooking pot.
2. Roughly chop onions and green pepper using a food processor.
3. Finely chop garlic, cilantro, and jalapeno peppers.
4. Sauté the chopped vegetables and add them to the pot.
5. Add the remaining ingredients (excluding spices) to the pot.
6. Cook over medium heat for about 1/2 hour, stirring frequently.
7. Add the spices one at a time, stirring well between each addition.
8. Simmer for another 1/2 hour, stirring frequently.
9. Adjust the spices to taste.
10. Simmer for another 1/2 hour, stirring frequently.
11. Again adjust the spices to taste.
This recipe makes about 10 quarts of mildly-spiced chili and serves about 20 people. Serve plain or over spaghetti, baked potato, or rice with grated low-fat cheese and chopped onions on the side. Tortilla chips or browned chunks of Italian bread are also good on the side. The chili freezes well. Freeze in plastic quart containers for quick 2 or 3 person meals. Approximately 100 calories per serving, 3 grams of fat, and 41 mg sodium.
